sub_modules.content: Signal transmitted downward using a horn antenna.

sub_modules.title: Beam Pattern
sub_modules.content: Elliptical beam ensures vertical reflection even with aircraft pitch and roll.

sub_modules.title: Receiver Antenna
sub_modules.content: Separate antenna receives reflected signal to avoid interference.

sub_modules.title: Radiated Power
sub_modules.content: Transmitted power is approximately one watt.

sub_modules.title: Height Measurement Principle
sub_modules.content: Height is calculated from shortest path directly below aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Indicator Scale
sub_modules.content: Logarithmic scale expanded from 0–500 ft and compressed above.

sub_modules.title: Mask Feature
sub_modules.content: Pointer disappears above 2500 ft, during faults, or when switched off.

sub_modules.title: Failure Warning Flag
sub_modules.content: Indicates signal issues, interference, or power failure.

sub_modules.title: Test Function
sub_modules.content: Test button moves pointer to preset value for system check.

sub_modules.title: Height Selector
sub_modules.content: Allows pilot to set decision height reference.

sub_modules.title: Low Height Warning
sub_modules.content: Visual and audio alerts when below selected height.

sub_modules.title: EFIS Display - Boeing
sub_modules.content: Digital display with color changes and scaling near decision height.

sub_modules.title: EFIS Display - Airbus
sub_modules.content: Height shown on PFD with color and size changes.

sub_modules.title: Autopilot Integration
sub_modules.content: Used to desensitize autopilot response during landing.

sub_modules.title: GPWS Integration
sub_modules.content: Provides height and rate data to ground proximity warning system.

sub_modules.title: Operating Range
sub_modules.content: Effective from 0 to 2500 feet AGL.

sub_modules.title: Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Accuracy is ±3% or ±1 ft, whichever is greater.

sub_modules.title: Error Sources
sub_modules.content: Includes Doppler shift, digital errors, and height lag.

sub_modules.title: Installation Delay
sub_modules.content: Compensation required for antenna position and cable length.

sub_modules.title: Residual Height
sub_modules.content: Difference between antenna position and wheel touchdown point.

sub_modules.title: Cable Length Compensation
sub_modules.content: Adjusts for signal delay due to long cable runs.

sub_modules.title: Touchdown Calibration
sub_modules.content: System calibrated to read zero at main wheel touchdown.

sub_modules.title: Negative Ground Reading
sub_modules.content: May show negative height on ground due to antenna position.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: FMS improves navigation, fuel efficiency, and reduces pilot workload using onboard computers.

sub_modules.title: Lateral Navigation (LNAV)
sub_modules.content: Controls aircraft along programmed route using waypoints and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Vertical Navigation (VNAV)
sub_modules.content: Optimizes climb, cruise, and descent profiles for efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Automation Role
sub_modules.content: FMS acts as a central control system for automatic or semi-automatic flight.

sub_modules.title: System Components
sub_modules.content: Includes CDU, FMC, FGS, ADC, IRS, and GPS.

sub_modules.title: Control and Display Unit (CDU)
sub_modules.content: Primary interface between pilot and FMS.

sub_modules.title: CDU Function
sub_modules.content: Allows pilot to input data and monitor flight parameters.

sub_modules.title: Multiple CDU Setup
sub_modules.content: Typically two CDUs are installed, sometimes three for redundancy.

sub_modules.title: Flight Management Computer (FMC)
sub_modules.content: Processes navigation and performance data.

sub_modules.title: Dual Mode Operation
sub_modules.content: Both FMCs operate simultaneously and cross-check data.

sub_modules.title: Single Mode Operation
sub_modules.content: One FMC continues operation if the other fails.

sub_modules.title: Independent Mode
sub_modules.content: FMCs operate independently without cross-checking.

sub_modules.title: Database Structure
sub_modules.content: FMC database includes performance and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Performance Database
sub_modules.content: Contains aircraft performance, drag, fuel, and speed data.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Database
sub_modules.content: Contains airports, runways, navaids, SIDs, STARs, and routes.

sub_modules.title: Database Cycle
sub_modules.content: Updated every 28 days to match navigation charts.

sub_modules.title: IDENT Page
sub_modules.content: Displays aircraft details and database information.

sub_modules.title: POS INIT Page
sub_modules.content: Used to initialize aircraft position and time.

sub_modules.title: RTE Page
sub_modules.content: Used to enter origin, destination, and route.

sub_modules.title: PERF INIT Page
sub_modules.content: Used to input weight, fuel, altitude, and cost index.

sub_modules.title: Cost Index
sub_modules.content: Balances fuel cost and time efficiency.

sub_modules.title: VNAV Climb
sub_modules.content: Controls climb using optimized speed and thrust.

sub_modules.title: Climb Constraints
sub_modules.content: Ensures compliance with altitude and speed restrictions.

sub_modules.title: Cruise Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains efficient cruise speed and altitude.

sub_modules.title: Top of Descent
sub_modules.content: Determines optimal point to begin descent.

sub_modules.title: Descent Profile
sub_modules.content: Calculates descent path based on constraints and efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Arrival and Approach
sub_modules.content: Guides aircraft to final approach or runway threshold.

sub_modules.title: LNAV Operation
sub_modules.content: Follows great circle routes and procedure paths.

sub_modules.title: Position Determination
sub_modules.content: Uses IRS, VOR, DME, and GPS inputs.

sub_modules.title: Position Error
sub_modules.content: Errors accumulate over time in inertial systems.

sub_modules.title: Error Monitoring
sub_modules.content: Pilot monitors differences between IRS systems.

sub_modules.title: System Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Typical accuracy better than 0.05 NM per hour.

sub_modules.title: GPS Integration
sub_modules.content: Improves overall navigation accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Alphanumeric Keys
sub_modules.content: Used to input data into the CDU scratchpad.

sub_modules.title: Function Keys
sub_modules.content: Access various system modes and data pages.

sub_modules.title: Line Select Keys
sub_modules.content: Used to transfer data between display and scratchpad.

sub_modules.title: EXEC Key
sub_modules.content: Activates entered data into the active flight plan.

sub_modules.title: CLR Key
sub_modules.content: Clears or edits scratchpad entries.

sub_modules.title: DEL Key
sub_modules.content: Deletes selected flight plan data.

sub_modules.title: Page Navigation Keys
sub_modules.content: Used to switch between display pages.

sub_modules.title: Climb Mode (CLB)
sub_modules.content: Displays and manages climb performance.

sub_modules.title: Cruise Mode (CRZ)
sub_modules.content: Displays cruise performance data.

sub_modules.title: Descent Mode (DES)
sub_modules.content: Displays descent performance data.

sub_modules.title: Route Page (RTE)
sub_modules.content: Handles flight plan route input and changes.

sub_modules.title: Legs Page (LEGS)
sub_modules.content: Displays detailed route segments.

sub_modules.title: Hold Function
sub_modules.content: Allows planning or execution of holding patterns.

sub_modules.title: Direct/Intercept
sub_modules.content: Allows direct navigation to a waypoint.

sub_modules.title: Fix Page
sub_modules.content: Displays bearing and distance to selected fixes.

sub_modules.title: Progress Page
sub_modules.content: Displays ETA, fuel, wind, and flight status.

sub_modules.title: Annunciators
sub_modules.content: Provide system status and warnings.

sub_modules.title: MSG Indicator
sub_modules.content: Indicates advisory or alert messages.

sub_modules.title: FAIL Indicator
sub_modules.content: Indicates FMC failure.

sub_modules.title: DISPLAY Indicator
sub_modules.content: Shows non-active flight plan data display.

sub_modules.title: OFFSET Indicator
sub_modules.content: Indicates parallel offset navigation.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: EFIS displays attitude and navigation data using electronic screens for better clarity and interpretation.

sub_modules.title: System Integration
sub_modules.content: Integrated with digital navigation systems using PFD and ND displays.

sub_modules.title: Display Technology
sub_modules.content: Uses CRT or LCD displays for presenting flight information.

sub_modules.title: System Configuration
sub_modules.content: Separate systems for Captain and First Officer.

sub_modules.title: Primary Units
sub_modules.content: Includes PFD (EADI), ND (EHSI), control panel, symbol generator, and light sensor.

sub_modules.title: Symbol Generator
sub_modules.content: Processes and distributes data signals to display units.

sub_modules.title: Backup Symbol Generator
sub_modules.content: Provides redundancy in case of failure.

sub_modules.title: Display Units
sub_modules.content: PFD and ND units are often interchangeable.

sub_modules.title: Colour Coding
sub_modules.content: Different colours represent priority and type of information.

sub_modules.title: White Colour
sub_modules.content: Represents primary flight information.

sub_modules.title: Green Colour
sub_modules.content: Indicates active system modes or lower priority data.

sub_modules.title: Magenta Colour
sub_modules.content: Indicates command or 'fly-to' guidance.

sub_modules.title: Cyan Colour
sub_modules.content: Represents background or non-active data.

sub_modules.title: Yellow Colour
sub_modules.content: Indicates caution or alerts.

sub_modules.title: Red Colour
sub_modules.content: Indicates severe warnings such as weather hazards.

sub_modules.title: Remote Light Sensor
sub_modules.content: Adjusts display brightness automatically based on ambient light.

sub_modules.title: Control Panel
sub_modules.content: Used to manage display modes and settings.

sub_modules.title: Brightness Control
sub_modules.content: Adjusts display brightness.

sub_modules.title: Decision Height Selector
sub_modules.content: Sets decision height for landing alerts.

sub_modules.title: Decision Height Reset
sub_modules.content: Resets DH alerts.

sub_modules.title: EADI Display
sub_modules.content: Shows pitch, roll, flight director, ILS data, and speed error.

sub_modules.title: Decision Height Concept
sub_modules.content: Minimum altitude for visual reference during landing.

sub_modules.title: Radio Altitude Display
sub_modules.content: Displayed digitally and graphically below 1000 ft.

sub_modules.title: DH Alerts
sub_modules.content: Visual and audio alerts triggered near decision height.

sub_modules.title: Speed Error Indicator
sub_modules.content: Shows deviation between commanded and actual speed.

sub_modules.title: EHSI Display
sub_modules.content: Displays navigation data in various modes.

sub_modules.title: Range Selector
sub_modules.content: Controls map and navigation display range.

sub_modules.title: Mode Selector
sub_modules.content: Switches between VOR, ILS, MAP, and PLAN modes.

sub_modules.title: Map Switches
sub_modules.content: Control display of navigation aids, airports, and routes.

sub_modules.title: Weather Radar Display
sub_modules.content: Shows weather intensity using color coding.

sub_modules.title: System Symbols
sub_modules.content: Includes waypoints, airports, navaids, and wind indicators.

sub_modules.title: Active Waypoint
sub_modules.content: Displayed in magenta as current navigation target.

sub_modules.title: Inactive Waypoint
sub_modules.content: Displayed in white as part of route.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Modes
sub_modules.content: Includes VOR, ILS, MAP, and PLAN display modes.

sub_modules.title: VOR Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays compass rose and VOR navigation data.

sub_modules.title: ILS Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays localizer and glide slope data.

sub_modules.title: MAP Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays moving map with route and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: PLAN Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays static map for route planning.

sub_modules.title: Compass Orientation
sub_modules.content: Displays heading or track relative to magnetic or true north.

sub_modules.title: Track and Heading Display
sub_modules.content: Shows aircraft heading and track information.

sub_modules.title: Wind Display
sub_modules.content: Shows wind speed and direction.

sub_modules.title: Weather Radar Integration
sub_modules.content: Displays precipitation intensity and turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Trend Vector
sub_modules.content: Predicts future aircraft position.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Arc
sub_modules.content: Shows predicted altitude achievement point.

sub_modules.title: Route Display
sub_modules.content: Displays active and inactive routes.

sub_modules.title: Offset Path
sub_modules.content: Shows parallel flight path offset.

sub_modules.title: Top of Climb/Descent
sub_modules.content: Indicates calculated climb and descent points.

sub_modules.title: Failure Indications
sub_modules.content: Displayed using flags and messages.

sub_modules.title: Warning Messages
sub_modules.content: Displayed for system discrepancies and failures.

sub_modules.title: Data Source Selection
sub_modules.content: Allows switching between different sensor inputs.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Symbols
sub_modules.content: Standard symbols represent navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Colour Priority System
sub_modules.content: Defines importance of displayed information.

sub_modules.title: System Advantages
sub_modules.content: Improves readability, reduces workload, and enhances situational awareness.

sub_modules.title: Definition of Computer
sub_modules.content: A computer is a device that stores data and executes programs to process that data.

sub_modules.title: Historical Background
sub_modules.content: The first general-purpose computer was the Z3 built by Konrad Zuse in 1941.

sub_modules.title: Types of Computers
sub_modules.content: Two main types: analogue and digital computers.

sub_modules.title: Analogue Computers
sub_modules.content: Use continuous variables like voltage or pressure to represent data.

sub_modules.title: Analogue Applications
sub_modules.content: Used for simulation of physical systems like aircraft structures.

sub_modules.title: Decline of Analogue Computers
sub_modules.content: Replaced by digital computers due to flexibility and programmability.

sub_modules.title: Digital Computers
sub_modules.content: Use binary data (0 and 1) for processing information.

sub_modules.title: Binary System
sub_modules.content: Represents data using two states: ON and OFF.

sub_modules.title: Digital Circuits
sub_modules.content: Operate as two-state switching systems.

sub_modules.title: Computer Hardware
sub_modules.content: Physical components of a computer system.

sub_modules.title: Central Processing Unit (CPU)
sub_modules.content: The brain of the computer that controls all operations.

sub_modules.title: Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU)
sub_modules.content: Performs arithmetic and logical operations.

sub_modules.title: Shift Registers
sub_modules.content: Temporary storage for data being processed.

sub_modules.title: Accumulator
sub_modules.content: Register that holds intermediate processing data.

sub_modules.title: Control Unit
sub_modules.content: Controls operations using timing signals from a clock.

sub_modules.title: Clock System
sub_modules.content: Provides timing pulses to synchronize operations.

sub_modules.title: Input Devices
sub_modules.content: Provide data to the computer from sensors or user input.

sub_modules.title: Output Devices
sub_modules.content: Display or transmit processed data.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Inputs
sub_modules.content: Sensors like radio altimeter, TAS, fuel flow.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Outputs
sub_modules.content: Displayed via EFIS or FMS CDU.

sub_modules.title: BIOS
sub_modules.content: Converts input/output signals into usable formats.

sub_modules.title: Working Memory
sub_modules.content: Used to store and execute programs.

sub_modules.title: Read Only Memory (ROM)
sub_modules.content: Stores permanent instructions programmed by manufacturer.

sub_modules.title: Programmable ROM
sub_modules.content: PROM, EPROM, EEPROM allow modification of stored programs.

sub_modules.title: Volatile Memory
sub_modules.content: Loses data when power is removed.

sub_modules.title: Non-Volatile Memory
sub_modules.content: Retains data without power.

sub_modules.title: Random Access Memory (RAM)
sub_modules.content: Allows read/write operations and stores active data.

sub_modules.title: Permanent Storage
sub_modules.content: Stores data long-term using various media.

sub_modules.title: Flash Memory
sub_modules.content: Fast, rewritable electronic storage.

sub_modules.title: Optical Storage
sub_modules.content: Uses CDs or similar media for data storage.

sub_modules.title: Magnetic Storage
sub_modules.content: Includes hard drives and older floppy disks.

sub_modules.title: Software
sub_modules.content: Programs that control computer operations.

sub_modules.title: Assembly Language
sub_modules.content: Low-level programming language tied to hardware.

sub_modules.title: High-Level Languages
sub_modules.content: User-friendly programming languages like C++, BASIC, FORTRAN.

sub_modules.title: Scripting Languages
sub_modules.content: Used to control applications and automate tasks.

sub_modules.title: Aviation Software Standards
sub_modules.content: Software must meet strict safety standards.

sub_modules.title: EUROCAE ED-12B
sub_modules.content: Defines safety levels for aviation software.

sub_modules.title: Software Level A
sub_modules.content: Catastrophic failure risk.

sub_modules.title: Software Level B
sub_modules.content: Hazardous or severe failure risk.

sub_modules.title: Software Level C
sub_modules.content: Major impact on operation.

sub_modules.title: Software Level D
sub_modules.content: Minor impact on safety.

sub_modules.title: Software Level E
sub_modules.content: No effect on safety.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Computer Systems
sub_modules.content: Used in FMS, DFGS, GPWS, TCAS, and fly-by-wire systems.

sub_modules.title: Dedicated Computers
sub_modules.content: Each system uses its own processor.

sub_modules.title: Future Integration
sub_modules.content: Trend toward centralized processing systems.

sub_modules.title: Integrated Hazard Warning System
sub_modules.content: Combines multiple warning systems into one.

sub_modules.title: Analogue to Digital Conversion
sub_modules.content: Converts analogue signals into digital form.

sub_modules.title: Digital to Analogue Conversion
sub_modules.content: Converts digital output into analogue signals.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Traditional ATC uses voice communication with radar in populated areas and procedural control in remote areas.

sub_modules.title: Limitations of Traditional ATC
sub_modules.content: HF communication issues and lack of radar lead to large separation distances and low traffic density.

sub_modules.title: Position Reporting
sub_modules.content: Aircraft report position periodically, often every 30–60 minutes in oceanic regions.

sub_modules.title: Need for Improvement
sub_modules.content: Increasing air traffic requires real-time accurate position and better communication systems.

sub_modules.title: GNSS Role
sub_modules.content: Provides highly accurate global positioning.

sub_modules.title: SATCOM Role
sub_modules.content: Enables global communication using satellites.

sub_modules.title: Communication Systems
sub_modules.content: Includes VHF, HF, and SATCOM communication methods.

sub_modules.title: VHF Communication
sub_modules.content: Line-of-sight communication limited to about 200 NM.

sub_modules.title: HF Communication
sub_modules.content: Long-range but unreliable due to interference.

sub_modules.title: SELCAL
sub_modules.content: Alerts pilots when messages are received without continuous listening.

sub_modules.title: SATCOM Communication
sub_modules.content: Provides global communication using satellites.

sub_modules.title: Voice Communication Limitations
sub_modules.content: Includes congestion, language issues, and limited channels.

sub_modules.title: Data Link Concept
sub_modules.content: Allows digital communication between aircraft and ground systems.

sub_modules.title: Data Link Advantages
sub_modules.content: Improves reliability, clarity, and speed of communication.

sub_modules.title: Uplink
sub_modules.content: Transmission from ground to aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Downlink
sub_modules.content: Transmission from aircraft to ground.

sub_modules.title: Data Link Providers
sub_modules.content: Includes SITA and ARINC.

sub_modules.title: CMU
sub_modules.content: Controls communication frequencies.

sub_modules.title: DCDU
sub_modules.content: Displays data link messages.

sub_modules.title: MCDU
sub_modules.content: Combined control and display unit.

sub_modules.title: ACARS
sub_modules.content: Data link system for communication between aircraft and ground.

sub_modules.title: ACARS Functions
sub_modules.content: Supports operational messages, maintenance data, and flight monitoring.

sub_modules.title: ATSU
sub_modules.content: Provides ATC services via data link.

sub_modules.title: ATSU Services
sub_modules.content: Includes flight information, alerting, advisory, and control services.

sub_modules.title: CNS/ATM
sub_modules.content: Integrated system combining communication, navigation, and surveillance.

sub_modules.title: ATM
sub_modules.content: Manages air traffic and operational efficiency.

sub_modules.title: ATC Data Link Messages
sub_modules.content: Includes departure clearance, oceanic clearance, and ATIS.

sub_modules.title: AOC Messages
sub_modules.content: Used by airlines for operational tracking.

sub_modules.title: OOOI Messages
sub_modules.content: Out, Off, On, In status updates.

sub_modules.title: Load Sheet
sub_modules.content: Provides aircraft weight and balance data.

sub_modules.title: Passenger Information
sub_modules.content: Supports cabin crew operations.

sub_modules.title: Weather Reports
sub_modules.content: Provides METAR, TAF, and SIGMET data.

sub_modules.title: Maintenance Reports
sub_modules.content: Reports aircraft system issues.

sub_modules.title: Free Text Messages
sub_modules.content: Allows flexible communication.

sub_modules.title: Logon Procedure
sub_modules.content: Pilot initiates connection with ATSU using ICAO address.

sub_modules.title: AFN
sub_modules.content: Air Traffic Service Facility Notification establishes connection.

sub_modules.title: FANS Concept
sub_modules.content: Uses GNSS and data link to provide global ATC coverage.

sub_modules.title: FANS Benefits
sub_modules.content: Reduces separation distances and improves efficiency.

sub_modules.title: FANS A
sub_modules.content: Used in oceanic and remote areas.

sub_modules.title: FANS B
sub_modules.content: Used in high-density airspace.

sub_modules.title: ADS
sub_modules.content: Automatic Dependent Surveillance provides aircraft position data.

sub_modules.title: ADS-C Contracts
sub_modules.content: Includes periodic, event-based, and emergency reporting.

sub_modules.title: CPDLC
sub_modules.content: Controller-Pilot Data Link Communication system.

sub_modules.title: CPDLC Messages
sub_modules.content: Structured messages for ATC instructions and responses.

sub_modules.title: FANS Architecture
sub_modules.content: Includes satellite, VHF, and ground systems.

sub_modules.title: ATN
sub_modules.content: Aeronautical Telecommunications Network for data exchange.

sub_modules.title: FANS Coverage
sub_modules.content: Global coverage using satellites and data links.

sub_modules.title: System Integration
sub_modules.content: Combines communication, navigation, and surveillance systems.

sub_modules.title: Safety Backup
sub_modules.content: TCAS provides collision avoidance.

sub_modules.title: Future Trends
sub_modules.content: Increasing automation and global integration of ATC systems.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: FDS provides steering and attitude guidance to reduce pilot workload and can interface with autopilot.

sub_modules.title: FDS Purpose
sub_modules.content: Integrates attitude, navigation, and flight path data to provide guidance commands.

sub_modules.title: FDS Channels
sub_modules.content: Consists of pitch channel and roll channel.

sub_modules.title: Data Sources
sub_modules.content: Includes ADC, VOR/ILS, FMS, and IRS inputs.

sub_modules.title: Attitude Information
sub_modules.content: Provided by gyro systems or IRS.

sub_modules.title: Electronic Attitude Director Indicator
sub_modules.content: Displays pitch, roll, and command bars.

sub_modules.title: Command Bars
sub_modules.content: Provide intuitive 'fly-to' guidance for pilot control.

sub_modules.title: Primary Flight Display
sub_modules.content: Combines attitude, speed, altitude, and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Flight Mode Annunciator
sub_modules.content: Displays active and armed modes of flight systems.

sub_modules.title: Electronic Horizontal Situation Indicator
sub_modules.content: Displays navigation and heading information.

sub_modules.title: Flight Director Computer
sub_modules.content: Processes inputs and generates guidance commands.

sub_modules.title: Symbol Generator
sub_modules.content: Displays processed data on EFIS screens.

sub_modules.title: Vertical Gyro
sub_modules.content: Provides attitude reference in older systems.

sub_modules.title: INS/IRS Integration
sub_modules.content: Modern systems use IRS instead of vertical gyro.

sub_modules.title: Mode Control Panel
sub_modules.content: Used by pilot to select FDS and autopilot modes.

sub_modules.title: Mode Annunciators
sub_modules.content: Indicate system status and mode engagement.

sub_modules.title: Raw Data
sub_modules.content: Unprocessed navigation signals like LOC and GS deviation.

sub_modules.title: Computed Data
sub_modules.content: Processed guidance commands from FDS.

sub_modules.title: FD Fail Indications
sub_modules.content: Flags indicate failures in sensors or system.

sub_modules.title: Gyro Failure Flag
sub_modules.content: Indicates failure of attitude reference.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Failure Flag
sub_modules.content: Indicates unreliable navigation signals.

sub_modules.title: Command Attitude
sub_modules.content: FDS provides pitch guidance for level, climb, or descent.

sub_modules.title: Take-off Mode
sub_modules.content: Activated by TO/GA switch providing initial pitch and roll commands.

sub_modules.title: Take-off Guidance
sub_modules.content: Commands pitch and speed targets during climb.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Hold
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected altitude using pitch commands.

sub_modules.title: Heading Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected heading.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Mode
sub_modules.content: Uses VOR, LOC, or FMS data for tracking.

sub_modules.title: LNAV
sub_modules.content: Uses FMS or GPS for lateral navigation.

sub_modules.title: ILS Approach Mode
sub_modules.content: Captures localizer and glide slope.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Capture
sub_modules.content: Aligns aircraft with runway centerline.

sub_modules.title: Glide Slope Capture
sub_modules.content: Controls descent along glide path.

sub_modules.title: Manual Glide Slope Mode
sub_modules.content: Allows capture from above if needed.

sub_modules.title: Go-Around Mode
sub_modules.content: Activated using TO/GA switch during missed approach.

sub_modules.title: Go-Around Guidance
sub_modules.content: Provides pitch and roll commands for climb.

sub_modules.title: Engine Failure Handling
sub_modules.content: Adjusts speed and pitch commands accordingly.

sub_modules.title: Manoeuvre Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents unsafe commands exceeding aircraft limits.

sub_modules.title: Gain Scheduling
sub_modules.content: Adjusts command sensitivity based on flight phase.

sub_modules.title: ILS Gain Adjustment
sub_modules.content: Reduces command intensity near runway.

sub_modules.title: Radio Altimeter Input
sub_modules.content: Used for accurate gain scheduling.

sub_modules.title: Dual FDS
sub_modules.content: Provides redundancy with two independent systems.

sub_modules.title: FD Comparator
sub_modules.content: Compares outputs of both FDS systems.

sub_modules.title: Comparator Function
sub_modules.content: Removes command bars if disagreement detected.

sub_modules.title: System Redundancy
sub_modules.content: Ensures continued operation if one system fails.

sub_modules.title: EFIS Integration
sub_modules.content: Displays FDS data on PFD and ND.

sub_modules.title: System Reliability
sub_modules.content: Includes backup systems and fail-safe features.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Autopilot reduces pilot workload and improves flight accuracy by controlling the aircraft automatically.

sub_modules.title: Purpose
sub_modules.content: Relieves fatigue and improves accuracy by reacting faster than human pilots.

sub_modules.title: Autothrottle Integration
sub_modules.content: Controls engine thrust automatically along with autopilot guidance.

sub_modules.title: Engagement
sub_modules.content: Autopilot can be engaged shortly after take-off, typically around 400 feet.

sub_modules.title: Response Time
sub_modules.content: Autopilot reacts faster than humans, improving stability and precision.

sub_modules.title: Fail Safe System
sub_modules.content: Limits actuator authority to allow pilot override during failures.

sub_modules.title: Basic Autopilot
sub_modules.content: Maintains aircraft attitude and stability.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Stabilization
sub_modules.content: Detects disturbances and applies corrective control automatically.

sub_modules.title: Inner Loop Control
sub_modules.content: Handles automatic stabilization through feedback control.

sub_modules.title: Inner Loop Functions
sub_modules.content: Includes sensing, computing, actuating, feedback, and correction.

sub_modules.title: Control Loops
sub_modules.content: Autopilot uses inner loop (stability) and outer loop (guidance).

sub_modules.title: Closed Loop System
sub_modules.content: Uses feedback to correct aircraft attitude continuously.

sub_modules.title: Open Loop System
sub_modules.content: Operates without feedback control.

sub_modules.title: Servo Mechanism
sub_modules.content: Amplifies small input signals into large control movements.

sub_modules.title: Attitude Sensor
sub_modules.content: Detects changes in aircraft attitude using gyros.

sub_modules.title: Transducer
sub_modules.content: Converts mechanical motion into electrical signals.

sub_modules.title: Signal Processor
sub_modules.content: Computes required corrective action.

sub_modules.title: Servomotor
sub_modules.content: Moves control surfaces based on computed signals.

sub_modules.title: Aerodynamic Feedback
sub_modules.content: Provides feedback from aircraft response.

sub_modules.title: Outer Loop Control
sub_modules.content: Provides guidance modes such as altitude hold and navigation.

sub_modules.title: Autopilot Axes
sub_modules.content: Controls roll, pitch, and yaw axes.

sub_modules.title: Single Axis System
sub_modules.content: Controls roll only (wing leveller).

sub_modules.title: Two Axis System
sub_modules.content: Controls roll and pitch.

sub_modules.title: Three Axis System
sub_modules.content: Controls roll, pitch, and yaw for full stability.

sub_modules.title: EU-OPS Requirements
sub_modules.content: Requires at least altitude hold and heading mode for IFR single pilot.

sub_modules.title: Actuator Types
sub_modules.content: Includes electromechanical, electrohydraulic, and pneumatic.

sub_modules.title: Parallel Actuator
sub_modules.content: Moves both control surface and control stick.

sub_modules.title: Series Actuator
sub_modules.content: Moves control surface only.

sub_modules.title: Torque Limiter
sub_modules.content: Prevents excessive loads and runaway conditions.

sub_modules.title: Autopilot Interlocks
sub_modules.content: Ensure safe engagement by verifying system integrity.

sub_modules.title: Engagement Conditions
sub_modules.content: Requires proper system state and no control input from pilot.

sub_modules.title: Disengagement Conditions
sub_modules.content: Triggered by failures, pilot input, or system faults.

sub_modules.title: Synchronization
sub_modules.content: Ensures smooth engagement without sudden control changes.

sub_modules.title: Trim System
sub_modules.content: Relieves control forces to maintain aircraft attitude.

sub_modules.title: Manual Trim
sub_modules.content: Operated by pilot using control inputs.

sub_modules.title: Auto Trim
sub_modules.content: Automatically adjusts trim during autopilot operation.

sub_modules.title: Trim Detection
sub_modules.content: Detects out-of-trim conditions using actuator load or position.

sub_modules.title: Trim Safety
sub_modules.content: Failure triggers autopilot disengagement and warnings.

sub_modules.title: Mach Trim
sub_modules.content: Prevents Mach tuck at high speeds.

sub_modules.title: Fly-by-Wire
sub_modules.content: Uses computers to control aircraft instead of direct mechanical input.

sub_modules.title: Flight Path Modes
sub_modules.content: Outer loop modes controlling aircraft trajectory.

sub_modules.title: Mode Control Panel
sub_modules.content: Interface for selecting autopilot modes.

sub_modules.title: Mode Types
sub_modules.content: Includes hold, capture, and tracking modes.

sub_modules.title: Roll Modes
sub_modules.content: Heading, VOR, LOC, LNAV.

sub_modules.title: Pitch Modes
sub_modules.content: Altitude hold, vertical speed, VNAV, speed hold.

sub_modules.title: Autoland
sub_modules.content: Fully automatic landing using all control axes.

sub_modules.title: Mode Annunciator
sub_modules.content: Displays autopilot status and active modes.

sub_modules.title: Sensor Inputs
sub_modules.content: Includes air data, attitude, heading, and navigation inputs.

sub_modules.title: Air Data
sub_modules.content: Provides altitude, speed, and vertical speed.

sub_modules.title: Attitude Reference
sub_modules.content: Provided by gyro or inertial systems.

sub_modules.title: Magnetic Heading
sub_modules.content: Provides heading reference to autopilot.

sub_modules.title: Radio Navigation
sub_modules.content: Provides VOR and ILS signals.

sub_modules.title: FMS Integration
sub_modules.content: Allows autopilot to follow programmed flight routes.

sub_modules.title: Heading Select Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected heading.

sub_modules.title: VOR/LOC Mode
sub_modules.content: Tracks radio navigation signals.

sub_modules.title: LNAV Mode
sub_modules.content: Follows programmed route using FMC.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Hold
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected altitude.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Acquire
sub_modules.content: Transitions to selected altitude.

sub_modules.title: Speed Hold
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected airspeed or Mach.

sub_modules.title: Vertical Speed Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected climb or descent rate.

sub_modules.title: Level Change Mode
sub_modules.content: Climbs or descends at selected speed.

sub_modules.title: VNAV Mode
sub_modules.content: Follows vertical profile from FMC.

sub_modules.title: Operational Phases
sub_modules.content: Different modes used for takeoff, climb, cruise, descent, and landing.

sub_modules.title: Control Wheel Steering
sub_modules.content: Allows pilot to adjust attitude without disengaging autopilot.

sub_modules.title: Touch Control Steering
sub_modules.content: Temporarily disengages autopilot for manual control.

sub_modules.title: Disengage Warning
sub_modules.content: Provides visual and aural alerts on autopilot disengagement.

sub_modules.title: Operational Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes pitch and roll limits for safe operation.

sub_modules.title: Gain Adaptation
sub_modules.content: Adjusts system sensitivity based on flight conditions.

sub_modules.title: Approach Mode
sub_modules.content: Handles ILS approach including localizer and glide slope.

sub_modules.title: Flight Management System
sub_modules.content: Integrates autopilot, navigation, and performance systems.

sub_modules.title: System Redundancy
sub_modules.content: Multiple systems ensure reliability and safety.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Autoland performs automatic landing by controlling aircraft in pitch, roll, yaw and thrust.

sub_modules.title: Landing Challenges
sub_modules.content: Requires alignment, controlled descent, speed reduction and drift correction.

sub_modules.title: Objective
sub_modules.content: Ensures safe landing without disturbance, with failure protection and accurate control.

sub_modules.title: System Requirements
sub_modules.content: Requires multiple autopilots, radio altimeters and CAT III ILS.

sub_modules.title: Fail Passive System
sub_modules.content: Handles failure safely but cannot complete autoland.

sub_modules.title: Fail Operational System
sub_modules.content: Continues autoland even after system failure.

sub_modules.title: Autoland Status
sub_modules.content: Displayed as LAND 2 (fail passive) or LAND 3 (fail operational).

sub_modules.title: Automatic Landing Sequence
sub_modules.content: Includes approach, capture, flare, touchdown and rollout phases.

sub_modules.title: Approach Mode
sub_modules.content: Arms localizer and glide slope capture.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Capture
sub_modules.content: Aligns aircraft with runway centerline.

sub_modules.title: Glide Slope Capture
sub_modules.content: Controls descent along glide path.

sub_modules.title: Dual Autopilot Operation
sub_modules.content: Required for full autoland capability.

sub_modules.title: Radio Altimeter
sub_modules.content: Provides accurate height above ground for landing phases.

sub_modules.title: Radio Altimeter Failure
sub_modules.content: Reduces system to fail-passive capability.

sub_modules.title: 1500 ft RA
sub_modules.content: Second autopilot engages and FLARE mode arms.

sub_modules.title: 800 ft RA
sub_modules.content: Second autopilot must be engaged for autoland.

sub_modules.title: 350 ft RA
sub_modules.content: If FLARE not armed, autopilot disengages.

sub_modules.title: Flare Mode
sub_modules.content: Reduces descent rate for smooth touchdown.

sub_modules.title: Thrust Reduction
sub_modules.content: Autothrottle reduces thrust to idle before touchdown.

sub_modules.title: Touchdown Phase
sub_modules.content: Aircraft pitch reduces and nose gear lowered.

sub_modules.title: Rollout Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains runway centerline after landing.

sub_modules.title: Runway Alignment
sub_modules.content: Yaw control removes drift (de-crabbing).

sub_modules.title: Go Around Mode
sub_modules.content: Allows automatic climb if landing aborted.

sub_modules.title: GA Pitch Control
sub_modules.content: Commands climb pitch and speed control.

sub_modules.title: GA Roll Control
sub_modules.content: Maintains ground track during go-around.

sub_modules.title: Autoland Completion
sub_modules.content: Ends when autopilot is disengaged after landing.

sub_modules.title: Single Autopilot Approach
sub_modules.content: Allows auto-approach but not full autoland.

sub_modules.title: ILS System
sub_modules.content: Provides localizer and glide slope guidance.

sub_modules.title: ILS Components
sub_modules.content: Includes ground transmitters and onboard receivers.

sub_modules.title: Localizer
sub_modules.content: Provides lateral guidance.

sub_modules.title: Glide Slope
sub_modules.content: Provides vertical guidance.

sub_modules.title: Marker Beacons
sub_modules.content: Provide distance information to runway.

sub_modules.title: Weather Minima
sub_modules.content: Defines minimum visibility and decision height.

sub_modules.title: Runway Visual Range
sub_modules.content: Indicates runway visibility distance.

sub_modules.title: Decision Height
sub_modules.content: Altitude where pilot must decide to land or go-around.

sub_modules.title: All Weather Operations
sub_modules.content: Allows landing in low visibility within limits.

sub_modules.title: Category 1
sub_modules.content: DH ≥ 200 ft and RVR ≥ 550 m.

sub_modules.title: Category 2
sub_modules.content: DH 100–200 ft and RVR ≥ 300 m.

sub_modules.title: Category 3A
sub_modules.content: DH < 100 ft and RVR ≥ 200 m.

sub_modules.title: Category 3B
sub_modules.content: DH < 50 ft and RVR ≥ 75 m.

sub_modules.title: Category 3C
sub_modules.content: Landing without visual reference.

sub_modules.title: Alert Height
sub_modules.content: Height where failures determine continuation or go-around.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Autothrottle automatically controls engine thrust using N1, EPR or target airspeed.

sub_modules.title: Purpose
sub_modules.content: Maintains required thrust or speed throughout all phases of flight.

sub_modules.title: Thrust Measurement
sub_modules.content: Thrust is indirectly measured using N1 or EPR values.

sub_modules.title: System Overview
sub_modules.content: Autothrottle is an electromechanical system integrated with autopilot and FMS.

sub_modules.title: Inputs
sub_modules.content: Includes ADC data, IRS data, engine sensors, AoA, radio altitude, MCP commands and flap position.

sub_modules.title: Outputs
sub_modules.content: Controls throttle servos, annunciations, warnings and engine displays.

sub_modules.title: Feedback
sub_modules.content: Compares actual and reference values to adjust thrust lever movement.

sub_modules.title: Power Management Control
sub_modules.content: PMC refines fuel flow electronically to maintain desired thrust.

sub_modules.title: PMC Failure
sub_modules.content: Failure triggers warnings and reverts control to mechanical system.

sub_modules.title: A/T Engagement
sub_modules.content: Activated by arming switch and selecting appropriate mode.

sub_modules.title: A/T Disengagement
sub_modules.content: Occurs due to switch action, faults, landing or thrust lever mismatch.

sub_modules.title: Take-off Mode
sub_modules.content: Engaged via TO/GA switch to set take-off thrust.

sub_modules.title: THR HLD Mode
sub_modules.content: Allows manual thrust adjustment during take-off roll.

sub_modules.title: Climb Transition
sub_modules.content: Switches from take-off thrust to climb thrust after lift-off.

sub_modules.title: N1 Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains engine thrust at selected N1 limit.

sub_modules.title: Speed Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains selected airspeed or Mach number.

sub_modules.title: FMC Speed Mode
sub_modules.content: Maintains speed commanded by FMC during VNAV.

sub_modules.title: N1 Equalization
sub_modules.content: Balances thrust between engines.

sub_modules.title: ARM Mode
sub_modules.content: System ready but not actively controlling thrust.

sub_modules.title: Descent Retard Mode
sub_modules.content: Reduces thrust during descent phases.

sub_modules.title: Landing Retard Mode
sub_modules.content: Automatically reduces thrust before touchdown.

sub_modules.title: Go-around Mode
sub_modules.content: Increases thrust for climb during missed approach.

sub_modules.title: GA Pitch and Roll Support
sub_modules.content: Supports climb and maintains track during go-around.

sub_modules.title: Disengage Switches
sub_modules.content: Manual switches allow pilot to disconnect autothrottle.

sub_modules.title: Warning Lights
sub_modules.content: Indicate disengagement or system errors.

sub_modules.title: Thrust Mode Annunciator
sub_modules.content: Displays active thrust mode and limits.

sub_modules.title: Flexible Take-off
sub_modules.content: Reduces engine wear by using reduced thrust for take-off.

sub_modules.title: Turbulence Operation
sub_modules.content: Autothrottle usable in light turbulence but not severe conditions.

sub_modules.title: FADEC Overview
sub_modules.content: Full Authority Digital Engine Control manages engine operations.

sub_modules.title: FADEC Functions
sub_modules.content: Controls fuel flow, engine limits, thrust and starting sequence.

sub_modules.title: FADEC Advantages
sub_modules.content: Improves efficiency, safety and reduces pilot workload.

sub_modules.title: FADEC Components
sub_modules.content: Includes EEC, sensors, fuel control units and interface systems.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Control
sub_modules.content: Regulates fuel flow using HMU and fuel metering valve.

sub_modules.title: Thrust Levers
sub_modules.content: Used for manual thrust input and FADEC reference.

sub_modules.title: Thrust Rating Limits
sub_modules.content: Defines maximum allowable thrust based on conditions.

sub_modules.title: Manual Mode
sub_modules.content: Pilot directly controls thrust using thrust levers.

sub_modules.title: Automatic Mode
sub_modules.content: Thrust controlled by FMGS within lever-defined limits.

sub_modules.title: Autothrust Inputs
sub_modules.content: Includes air data, engine data and pilot commands.

sub_modules.title: Autothrust Outputs
sub_modules.content: Controls fuel system and engine thrust parameters.

sub_modules.title: Dutch Roll
sub_modules.content: Oscillation caused by interaction between yaw and roll stability.

sub_modules.title: Cause of Dutch Roll
sub_modules.content: Excess lateral stability with low directional stability leads to oscillations.

sub_modules.title: Yaw-Roll Interaction
sub_modules.content: Yaw disturbance creates roll and vice versa due to lift and drag differences.

sub_modules.title: Effect of Swept Wings
sub_modules.content: Swept wings increase Dutch roll tendency due to lift variations.

sub_modules.title: Dynamic Stability
sub_modules.content: Damping reduces oscillations; effectiveness decreases at high altitude.

sub_modules.title: Function of Yaw Damper
sub_modules.content: Automatically applies rudder to counter yaw and prevent Dutch roll.

sub_modules.title: System Integration
sub_modules.content: Yaw damper is part of autopilot or an additional control system.

sub_modules.title: Operational Role
sub_modules.content: Provides turn coordination, runway alignment and asymmetric thrust support.

sub_modules.title: Multiple Systems
sub_modules.content: Modern aircraft use multiple yaw dampers for redundancy.

sub_modules.title: Sensing
sub_modules.content: Yaw rate is detected using rate gyro or IRS inputs.

sub_modules.title: Phase Advance
sub_modules.content: Applies correction at peak yaw rate to prevent overshoot.

sub_modules.title: Dutch Roll Filter
sub_modules.content: Allows only Dutch roll frequency signals to control rudder.

sub_modules.title: Filter Function
sub_modules.content: Blocks normal turn inputs to avoid interfering with pilot commands.

sub_modules.title: Signal Processing
sub_modules.content: Processes gyro input to generate corrective rudder commands.

sub_modules.title: Rudder Authority
sub_modules.content: Limited rudder movement (3–6 degrees) to prevent overcontrol.

sub_modules.title: Split Rudder Systems
sub_modules.content: Multiple yaw dampers share control for redundancy.

sub_modules.title: Gain Scheduling
sub_modules.content: Adjusts rudder authority based on flight conditions.

sub_modules.title: Synchronization Mode
sub_modules.content: Prevents transients during yaw damper engagement.

sub_modules.title: Engaged Mode
sub_modules.content: Processes signals and commands rudder movement.

sub_modules.title: Feedback System
sub_modules.content: Uses position feedback to stabilize rudder movement.

sub_modules.title: Neutral Position Control
sub_modules.content: Ensures rudder returns to center after correction.

sub_modules.title: Yaw Damper Testing
sub_modules.content: Simulates yaw to verify system operation.

sub_modules.title: Test Indications
sub_modules.content: Rudder indicator moves and returns to center if system is working.

sub_modules.title: System Indications
sub_modules.content: Includes switches, test buttons and failure lights.

sub_modules.title: Failure Indications
sub_modules.content: Alerts for power loss, sensor failure or system faults.

sub_modules.title: Rudder Trim Indicator
sub_modules.content: Shows rudder movement due to yaw damper activity.

sub_modules.title: Taxi Test
sub_modules.content: Yaw damper operation can be verified during aircraft turns on ground.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Autopilot must control aircraft safely within performance and structural limits.

sub_modules.title: Control Logic
sub_modules.content: Control inputs are proportional to deviation but limited to safe boundaries.

sub_modules.title: Flight Envelope Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes speed, pitch, bank angle, load factor and structural limits.

sub_modules.title: Command Speed Limiting
sub_modules.content: Prevents aircraft from exceeding speed and configuration limits.

sub_modules.title: Speed Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes VMO/MMO, flap limits, gear limits and minimum speed.

sub_modules.title: Minimum Speed
sub_modules.content: Approximately 1.3 VS based on angle of attack.

sub_modules.title: Limit Enforcement
sub_modules.content: System restricts speed commands to remain within limits.

sub_modules.title: Overspeed Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents exceeding maximum allowable speeds.

sub_modules.title: Underspeed Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents aircraft from stalling by maintaining minimum speed.

sub_modules.title: Reversion Modes
sub_modules.content: System switches modes to maintain safe flight conditions.

sub_modules.title: Placard Limit Reversion
sub_modules.content: Reverts to speed control to avoid exceeding limits.

sub_modules.title: Minimum Speed Reversion
sub_modules.content: Reverts to maintain safe speed if nearing stall conditions.

sub_modules.title: Pitch Control in Reversion
sub_modules.content: Commands nose-down pitch to increase airspeed.

sub_modules.title: Flight Envelope Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents aircraft from exceeding safe operational limits.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of Protection
sub_modules.content: Ensures safety during extreme conditions and pilot errors.

sub_modules.title: Protected Parameters
sub_modules.content: Angle of attack, speed, pitch, bank angle and load factor.

sub_modules.title: High Angle of Attack Protection
sub_modules.content: Limits AoA to prevent stall and activates thrust if needed.

sub_modules.title: High Speed Protection
sub_modules.content: Adds nose-up demand to prevent excessive speed.

sub_modules.title: Pitch Attitude Protection
sub_modules.content: Limits pitch to 30° nose-up and 15° nose-down.

sub_modules.title: Bank Angle Protection
sub_modules.content: Limits roll angle and stabilizes aircraft automatically.

sub_modules.title: Load Factor Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents structural overload by limiting g-forces.

sub_modules.title: Fly-by-Wire Protection
sub_modules.content: Advanced systems enforce full flight envelope protection.

sub_modules.title: Pilot Authority
sub_modules.content: Allows full control while preventing unsafe actions.

sub_modules.title: Autopilot Gain Scheduling
sub_modules.content: Adjusts control sensitivity based on flight phase.

sub_modules.title: Gain Adaptation
sub_modules.content: Reduces control authority near ground and at high speeds.

sub_modules.title: Approach Phase Limits
sub_modules.content: Autopilot authority reduced during landing and approach.

sub_modules.title: Altitude and Speed Effects
sub_modules.content: Control limits vary with altitude and aircraft performance.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Flight Warning System (FWS) produces cautions and warnings to increase crew situational awareness and provide indications of actions required to avoid danger. Due to multiple warning systems, an integrated system prioritizes warnings relevant to each phase of flight.

sub_modules.title: Levels of Alerts
sub_modules.content: Alerts are classified into three levels: Level A (Warnings) require immediate action, Level B (Cautions) require immediate awareness and subsequent action, and Level C (Advisories) require crew awareness.

sub_modules.title: Warnings in General
sub_modules.content: Warnings and alerts are presented in visual, aural, and sensory (tactile) forms to ensure effective communication to the crew.

sub_modules.title: Visual Warnings
sub_modules.content: Visual alerts use colour coding: red for warnings, amber/yellow for cautions, and other colours for advisories. These are displayed on electronic screens or via lights and flags. Master warning and caution lights are placed in the pilot’s central field of view.

sub_modules.title: Aural Warnings
sub_modules.content: Aural warnings include sounds and voice alerts. Warnings are prioritized (e.g., Stall, Windshear, GPWS, ACAS). Boeing uses bells, sirens, and voice alerts, while Airbus uses chimes, tones, and synthetic voice alerts.

sub_modules.title: Caution Alerts
sub_modules.content: Cautions are indicated by beepers, tones, or chimes to alert the crew of potential safety threats requiring attention.

sub_modules.title: Sensory (Tactile) Warnings
sub_modules.content: Tactile warnings include stick shaker and stick pusher systems to warn of stall conditions and assist in recovery.

sub_modules.title: Master Warning Systems
sub_modules.content: Master warning indicators and centralized warning panels (or EFIS displays in modern aircraft) present warnings in a structured and prioritized manner.

sub_modules.title: Flight Warning System Functions
sub_modules.content: The FWS generates warnings for engine and airframe malfunctions, aerodynamic limits exceeded, and external hazards such as terrain or traffic.

sub_modules.title: Aerodynamic Limit Warnings
sub_modules.content: Warnings include altitude alerts, overspeed warnings, and stall warnings when flight limits are exceeded.

sub_modules.title: External Hazard Warnings
sub_modules.content: Hazards such as terrain and traffic are managed using systems like GPWS and ACAS.

sub_modules.title: FWS Components
sub_modules.content: The system consists of inputs (sensors, GPWS, ACAS), processing units (flight warning computers), and outputs (visual and aural alerts and warnings).

sub_modules.title: Outputs
sub_modules.content: Outputs include visual alerts (lights, text), aural alerts (tones, voice), and attention-getting indicators such as glare shield lights.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Flight Warning System alerts the crew of deviations from aerodynamic parameters such as altitude, airspeed, and angle of attack. It provides altitude alerting, overspeed warning, and stall warning.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Alerting System - Function
sub_modules.content: The system warns pilots when approaching or deviating from a selected altitude set on the autopilot control panel within defined height bands.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Alerting System - Operation
sub_modules.content: Typical alert bands are 300–900 ft (Boeing) and 250–750 ft (Airbus). Alerts include visual indications and audible warnings when approaching or deviating from selected altitude.

sub_modules.title: Approaching Selected Altitude
sub_modules.content: At 900 ft before the selected altitude, a white box appears around altitude display with an audible alert. At 300 ft, the box disappears.

sub_modules.title: Deviation from Selected Altitude
sub_modules.content: At 300 ft deviation, master caution lights illuminate, a continuous beeper sounds, EICAS shows ALTITUDE ALERT, and altitude box turns amber.

sub_modules.title: System Inhibition
sub_modules.content: Altitude alerting is inhibited when glide slope is captured or when landing flaps are selected with gear down.

sub_modules.title: Regulatory Requirement
sub_modules.content: EASA requires altitude alerting systems in large turboprop and all turbojet aircraft. The system must alert when approaching and deviating from selected altitude.

sub_modules.title: Overspeed Warning - Function
sub_modules.content: Warns crew when airspeed exceeds VMO/MMO limits calculated by the Air Data Computer.

sub_modules.title: Overspeed Warning - Operation
sub_modules.content: Triggers siren/horn, red master warning lights, and OVERSPEED message on EICAS. Warning continues until condition is corrected.

sub_modules.title: Overspeed Displays
sub_modules.content: Maximum speed is shown by a barber’s pole on ASI or airspeed tape on PFD. As altitude increases, MMO limit reduces in indicated airspeed.

sub_modules.title: Stall Warning System - Function
sub_modules.content: Warns the pilot of an impending stall when angle of attack approaches critical value.

sub_modules.title: Stall Warning Systems
sub_modules.content: Simple systems use vane sensors; advanced systems include stick-shaker and stick-pusher mechanisms.

sub_modules.title: Stall Warning Indications
sub_modules.content: Warnings may be tactile, aural, visual, or combined. Examples include stick shaker vibration, voice alerts, and warning lights.

sub_modules.title: Stall Warning Operation
sub_modules.content: Inputs include angle of attack, flap/slat position, landing gear status, and airspeed. System activates during flight conditions such as take-off.

sub_modules.title: Stall Protection
sub_modules.content: Systems prevent stall using flight computers, automatic thrust increase, or stick-pusher mechanisms.

sub_modules.title: Angle of Attack (AoA)
sub_modules.content: AoA is the angle between wing chord line and airflow. Lift increases with AoA until critical angle is reached, beyond which stall occurs.

sub_modules.title: Angle of Attack Sensors
sub_modules.content: Sensors include conical probes and vane detectors mounted on fuselage, often heated to prevent icing.

sub_modules.title: Angle of Attack Indicators
sub_modules.content: AoA indicators may be provided to give pilots continuous awareness of aerodynamic conditions.

sub_modules.title: Configuration Warning
sub_modules.content: Warns if aircraft is not in proper configuration for take-off or landing, such as incorrect flap setting or gear status.

sub_modules.title: Take-off Configuration Warning
sub_modules.content: Triggers warnings if take-off is attempted with improper configuration such as flaps/slats not set, trim incorrect, spoilers deployed, or parking brake engaged.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Ground Proximity Warning System (GPWS) provides visual and aural warnings when terrain proximity threatens aircraft safety. It enhances safety by preventing accidents due to crew error, navigation issues, or ATC instructions.

sub_modules.title: System Overview
sub_modules.content: GPWS consists of inputs, outputs, and a central processing unit. It operates between 50 ft and 2450 ft above ground and automatically selects the appropriate warning mode.

sub_modules.title: Definitions
sub_modules.content: Alert: A caution generated by EGPWS. Warning: A command generated by EGPWS.

sub_modules.title: Types of Alerts
sub_modules.content: Alerts can be Genuine (correct operation), Nuisance (correct but unnecessary in safe conditions), or False (due to system fault).

sub_modules.title: GPWS Operating Modes Overview
sub_modules.content: GPWS has multiple modes to detect hazardous conditions including excessive descent rate, terrain closure, altitude loss, unsafe configuration, glide slope deviation, minimum altitude breach, and windshear.

sub_modules.title: Mode 1 - Excessive Descent Rate
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'SINK RATE' alert and 'WHOOP WHOOP PULL UP' warning when descent rate exceeds safe limits.

sub_modules.title: Mode 2 - Excessive Terrain Closure Rate
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'TERRAIN TERRAIN' alert followed by 'WHOOP WHOOP PULL UP' warning when terrain closure rate is unsafe.

sub_modules.title: Mode 3 - Altitude Loss After Take-off
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'DON'T SINK' alert when altitude decreases after take-off or go-around.

sub_modules.title: Mode 4A - Unsafe Terrain Clearance (Gear Up)
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'TOO LOW GEAR' or 'TOO LOW TERRAIN' when flying too low without landing gear deployed.

sub_modules.title: Mode 4B - Unsafe Terrain Clearance (Flaps)
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'TOO LOW FLAPS' or 'TOO LOW TERRAIN' when flaps are not in landing configuration.

sub_modules.title: Mode 5 - Glide Slope Deviation
sub_modules.content: Triggers 'GLIDE SLOPE' alert when aircraft descends below ILS glide path.

sub_modules.title: Mode 6 - Minimum Altitude and Bank Angle
sub_modules.content: Includes 'MINIMUMS' alert and bank angle warnings, as well as altitude call-outs.

sub_modules.title: Mode 7 - Windshear Warning
sub_modules.content: Provides windshear warnings based on flight parameter changes. Takes priority over all other modes.

sub_modules.title: Pilot Response to Warnings
sub_modules.content: Immediate action is required: level wings and initiate maximum climb until safe altitude is reached. Investigation of cause is secondary.

sub_modules.title: Enhanced GPWS (EGPWS)
sub_modules.content: EGPWS uses terrain databases and navigation systems to provide look-ahead warnings and terrain displays, improving safety over basic GPWS.

sub_modules.title: Terrain Display
sub_modules.content: Displays terrain ahead using color coding (green, yellow, red) and provides early warnings such as 'Terrain Ahead' or 'Pull Up'.

sub_modules.title: Terrain Clearance Floor
sub_modules.content: Prevents premature descent during approaches by monitoring aircraft position relative to runway.

sub_modules.title: Integrity Testing
sub_modules.content: GPWS includes Built-In Test Equipment (BITE) for preflight testing and continuous in-flight monitoring.

sub_modules.title: Mode Inhibition
sub_modules.content: Certain GPWS modes can be inhibited under specific conditions such as non-standard approaches or equipment configurations.

chapter_name: Chapter 35 - Airborne Collision and Avoidance System

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Airborne Collision Avoidance System (ACAS), also known as TCAS, provides an independent safety system to prevent mid-air collisions. It operates independently of ATC using aircraft transponders.

sub_modules.title: TCAS Systems Overview
sub_modules.content: Four versions exist (I–IV). TCAS I provides traffic awareness only, while TCAS II provides both Traffic Advisories (TAs) and Resolution Advisories (RAs).

sub_modules.title: TCAS I
sub_modules.content: Provides visual and aural traffic alerts ('Traffic, Traffic') but does not provide avoidance instructions.

sub_modules.title: TCAS II
sub_modules.content: Provides both Traffic Advisories (TAs) and Resolution Advisories (RAs) such as climb or descend commands to avoid collisions.

sub_modules.title: Principle of Operation
sub_modules.content: TCAS operates using SSR radar frequencies (1030/1090 MHz) and creates protective 3D zones around the aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Equipment Requirements
sub_modules.content: Intruder aircraft must have at least Mode A transponder. Mode C provides altitude, and Mode S allows coordinated avoidance via data link.

sub_modules.title: Operation
sub_modules.content: Range is determined by time delay of signals, bearing by directional antenna, and altitude by comparing Mode C data.

sub_modules.title: System Interconnections
sub_modules.content: TCAS integrates with transponders, displays (PFD, ND, VSI), radio altimeter, ADC, and IRS. It also considers aircraft performance and configuration.

sub_modules.title: Synthetic Voice Prioritization
sub_modules.content: Warning priorities are: Stall > Windshear > GPWS > TCAS.

sub_modules.title: Traffic Advisories (TAs)
sub_modules.content: Generated when intruder enters outer zone (45–35 seconds to collision). Displayed as amber circles with 'Traffic, Traffic' alert.

sub_modules.title: Resolution Advisories (RAs)
sub_modules.content: Generated when intruder enters inner zone (35–15 seconds to collision). Displayed as red symbols with commands like 'Climb' or 'Descend'.

sub_modules.title: Types of Resolution Advisories
sub_modules.content: Preventative RAs: 'Monitor Vertical Speed'. Corrective RAs: require climb or descent manoeuvres.

sub_modules.title: Traffic Categories
sub_modules.content: Proximate traffic (cyan solid diamond) and other traffic (hollow diamond) are displayed for situational awareness.

sub_modules.title: Data Tag Information
sub_modules.content: Displays relative altitude and climb/descent trends using arrows and numerical values.

sub_modules.title: Off Scale and No Bearing Alerts
sub_modules.content: Traffic outside display range or without bearing is still shown using partial symbols or text alerts.

sub_modules.title: TCAS Displays
sub_modules.content: Information is shown on PPI, VSI, and navigation displays (ND) in modern aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Control Panel Functions
sub_modules.content: Includes modes: Standby, On, TA, RA/TA, and Test. Range selection controls display scale.

sub_modules.title: TCAS Modes
sub_modules.content: TA mode provides advisories only; RA/TA mode provides full collision avoidance guidance.

sub_modules.title: TCAS Testing
sub_modules.content: Built-in test equipment (BITE) verifies system functionality with voice feedback indicating success or failure.

sub_modules.title: Action on Traffic Advisory
sub_modules.content: Pilots should visually search for traffic and prepare for possible RA but not manoeuvre based solely on TA.

sub_modules.title: Action on Resolution Advisory
sub_modules.content: Pilots must immediately follow RA commands, adjusting flight path accordingly and informing ATC.

sub_modules.title: Priority over ATC
sub_modules.content: TCAS RA instructions take priority over ATC instructions if they conflict.

sub_modules.title: Disregarding RAs
sub_modules.content: RAs may only be disregarded if visual confirmation ensures no collision risk exists.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Flight Data Recorder (FDR) records aircraft parameters throughout the flight to help determine causes of accidents, support trend analysis, and assist troubleshooting. In smaller aircraft, it may be combined with the Cockpit Voice Recorder.

sub_modules.title: FDR Designs
sub_modules.content: The FDR records 10 or 25 hours of data on a digital storage device housed in a shock-resistant box located at the rear of the aircraft. It includes an underwater locating device (ULD).

sub_modules.title: FDR Components
sub_modules.content: Components include a recording system, overhead control unit, pedestal control unit, and Data Interface and Acquisition Unit (DIAU).

sub_modules.title: Data Processing and Systems Integration
sub_modules.content: Data from DIAU is sent to the Aeroplane Condition Monitoring System (ACMS) along with inputs from various aircraft systems for monitoring and analysis.

sub_modules.title: Control Units
sub_modules.content: The overhead panel controls CVR and DFDR with ON and AUTO modes. The pedestal has an EVENT push-button to mark significant events in recordings.

sub_modules.title: Automatic Operation
sub_modules.content: The FDR starts when the first engine starts and stops automatically 5 minutes after the last engine shutdown.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Integrated Data Systems (AIDS)
sub_modules.content: AIDS processes aircraft system data using a Data Management Unit (DMU) and sends selected data to the FDR and maintenance systems. Data can be transmitted via ACARS.

sub_modules.title: Parameters Recorded
sub_modules.content: Mandatory parameters include time, attitude, airspeed, altitude, heading, acceleration, engine power, control positions, and configuration states.

sub_modules.title: Additional Parameters
sub_modules.content: Additional data includes flight control positions, radio altitude, navigation data, cockpit warnings, and landing gear position.

sub_modules.title: Regulatory Requirements
sub_modules.content: FDR carriage is mandatory based on aircraft weight, passenger capacity, and registration date as per European regulations.

sub_modules.title: Recording Duration Requirements
sub_modules.content: Aircraft over 5700 kg must record at least 25 hours. Smaller turbine aircraft with more than 9 passengers must record at least 10 hours.

sub_modules.title: Additional Recording Requirements
sub_modules.content: Aircraft over 27000 kg MTOM must record additional parameters beyond the basic set.

sub_modules.title: Operational Requirements
sub_modules.content: FDR must start before aircraft movement and stop after it ceases movement. It must include a water-locating device.

sub_modules.title: Combined Recorders
sub_modules.content: Aircraft under 5700 kg may combine FDR and CVR, while larger aircraft must have separate or dual combined systems.

sub_modules.title: Dispatch with Inoperative FDR
sub_modules.content: Aircraft may operate with an inoperative FDR under strict conditions: limited flights, time restrictions, and functional CVR.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: The Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) preserves critical flight deck audio for accident investigation. It records the last 30 minutes (or up to 2 hours) of cockpit communications and sounds.

sub_modules.title: System Operation
sub_modules.content: The CVR operates automatically when AC power is available, engines are running, or the aircraft is airborne.

sub_modules.title: Recording Requirements
sub_modules.content: The CVR records from before the aircraft moves under its own power until after it stops, including pre-start and post-shutdown phases.

sub_modules.title: Parameters Recorded
sub_modules.content: Recorded data includes cockpit voice communications, ambient sounds, interphone communications, headset audio, and PA system communications.

sub_modules.title: Voice Recorder Unit
sub_modules.content: The recorder is housed in a crash-resistant, high-temperature and fire-resistant case located at the rear of the aircraft. It includes an underwater locating device (ULD).

sub_modules.title: Underwater Locating Device (ULD)
sub_modules.content: The ULD emits ultrasonic signals when submerged to aid recovery and operates automatically with a battery lasting several days.

sub_modules.title: Control Unit
sub_modules.content: Mounted on the flight deck, the control unit includes monitoring systems and an area microphone to capture cockpit audio.

sub_modules.title: Control Modes
sub_modules.content: AUTO mode starts recording with engine start and stops after shutdown. ON mode starts recording immediately until engine start resets it to AUTO.

sub_modules.title: Testing (BITE)
sub_modules.content: The TEST function performs system checks using built-in test equipment and provides a visual indication of system status.

sub_modules.title: Erase Function
sub_modules.content: Erasure is only possible on the ground with engines off and parking brake set. Safety interlocks prevent accidental erasure.

sub_modules.title: European Regulations
sub_modules.content: CVR carriage requirements depend on aircraft weight, passenger capacity, and registration date, similar to FDR regulations.

sub_modules.title: Recording Duration Requirements
sub_modules.content: Minimum recording time is 30 minutes, or 2 hours for aircraft over 5700 kg registered after April 1998.

sub_modules.title: Combined Recorders
sub_modules.content: Aircraft under 5700 kg may use combined CVR/FDR units. Larger aircraft may also use combined systems but must have redundancy.

sub_modules.title: Dispatch with Inoperative CVR
sub_modules.content: Dispatch rules for unserviceable CVR are similar to FDR, with limitations on duration and operational conditions.

sub_modules.title: Introduction to Engine Instruments
sub_modules.content: Engine instruments are essential for safe aircraft operation. They are divided into performance indicators (e.g. EPR, N1) and engine condition indicators (e.g. EGT, oil pressure, temperature).

sub_modules.title: Types of Display
sub_modules.content: Displays are either analogue (clockwork cockpit) or electronic (glass cockpit using CRT, LCD, LED). Modern aircraft predominantly use glass cockpits.

sub_modules.title: Thrust and Power Measurement
sub_modules.content: Thrust is measured using P7 pressure or EPR (ratio of jet pipe to intake pressure). Propeller aircraft measure torque as an indication of power.

sub_modules.title: Engine Pressure Ratio (EPR)
sub_modules.content: EPR is derived from pressure ratios using sensors and transducers. It varies with airspeed due to intake pressure changes and must be set carefully during take-off.

sub_modules.title: Engine Torque Measurement
sub_modules.content: Torque indicates engine power and is measured either using oil pressure systems or electronic shaft displacement systems.

sub_modules.title: Oil Torquemeter System
sub_modules.content: Uses axial thrust from helical gears balanced by oil pressure to determine torque output.

sub_modules.title: Electronic Torquemeter
sub_modules.content: Uses phase shift between rotating shafts detected via electromagnetic pickups to measure torque.

sub_modules.title: Engine RPM Measurement
sub_modules.content: Engine speed is measured using tachometers. Methods include mechanical, electrical generator, and inductive probe systems.

sub_modules.title: Mechanical Tachometer
sub_modules.content: Uses rotating magnet and drag cup with eddy currents to drive a pointer against a spring.

sub_modules.title: Electrical Tachometer System
sub_modules.content: Uses a generator driven by the engine to produce signals interpreted by an indicator.

sub_modules.title: Inductive Speed Probe
sub_modules.content: Uses magnetic flux changes from rotating components to generate frequency signals proportional to engine speed.

sub_modules.title: Speed Indications
sub_modules.content: Speed is typically shown as a percentage of maximum (N1, N2, N3). Indicators include colour-coded limits and digital displays.

sub_modules.title: Synchroscope
sub_modules.content: Used to synchronize engine speeds in multi-engine aircraft to reduce vibration and noise.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Measurement
sub_modules.content: Temperature monitoring is critical to prevent engine damage. Systems measure air, oil, and turbine temperatures.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Measurement Types
sub_modules.content: Includes expansion type, vapour pressure type, electrical (resistance and thermocouple), and radiation (pyrometry).

sub_modules.title: Thermocouple System
sub_modules.content: Uses two dissimilar metals to generate voltage proportional to temperature (Seebeck effect). Used for EGT/TGT measurements.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Probe Placement
sub_modules.content: Multiple probes are placed around the engine to average readings and improve reliability.

sub_modules.title: Air Temperature Measurement
sub_modules.content: Includes SAT, RAT, and TAT. Ram rise affects readings at high speeds and requires correction.

sub_modules.title: Pressure Measurement
sub_modules.content: Pressure is measured using direct or remote systems. Units include psi, inHg, bar, and pascal.

sub_modules.title: Pressure Sensing Elements
sub_modules.content: Includes diaphragms, capsules, bellows, and Bourdon tubes, each suited for different pressure ranges.

sub_modules.title: Remote Indicating Systems
sub_modules.content: Use transmitters and receivers to measure pressure remotely, reducing weight and hazards.

sub_modules.title: Engine Vibration Monitoring
sub_modules.content: Uses piezoelectric or magnetic sensors to detect abnormal vibration indicating engine damage.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Quantity Measurement
sub_modules.content: Fuel can be measured by volume or mass. Mass measurement is preferred in modern aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Float Type Fuel Gauge
sub_modules.content: Uses a float and variable resistor system but is prone to errors due to tank shape and aircraft attitude.

sub_modules.title: Capacitance Fuel System
sub_modules.content: Uses capacitors with fuel as dielectric to measure fuel quantity accurately and compensate for temperature changes.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Flow Measurement
sub_modules.content: Measures rate of fuel consumption using turbine or pressure-based systems. Can indicate both flow rate and total consumption.

sub_modules.title: Remote Signal Transmission
sub_modules.content: Uses transmitters and receivers to indicate positions of engine controls remotely.

sub_modules.title: Flight Hour Meter
sub_modules.content: Records engine and system usage, typically activated by weight-on-wheels or airspeed switches.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Electronic instrumentation displays engine and airframe system parameters using CRT or LCD displays. Systems include EICAS (Boeing) and ECAM (Airbus).

sub_modules.title: EICAS and ECAM Overview
sub_modules.content: EICAS focuses on engine data and alerts, while ECAM integrates system monitoring with checklist and schematic displays.

sub_modules.title: EICAS System Architecture
sub_modules.content: EICAS consists of display units, control panel, and dual computers (left and right) with automatic or manual switching.

sub_modules.title: Display Units
sub_modules.content: Upper display shows primary engine parameters (N1, EGT, alerts). Lower display shows secondary parameters (N2, fuel flow, oil data, vibration, system status).

sub_modules.title: Display Colour Coding
sub_modules.content: White: normal data, Red: warnings, Green: thrust settings, Blue: test, Yellow: cautions/advisories, Magenta: special operations, Cyan: labels and status.

sub_modules.title: Display Modes
sub_modules.content: EICAS operates in three modes: operational, status, and maintenance.

sub_modules.title: Operational Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays primary engine data and alerts. Secondary data appears on demand.

sub_modules.title: Status Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays aircraft system readiness, flight control positions, and system status for dispatch checks.

sub_modules.title: Maintenance Mode
sub_modules.content: Provides troubleshooting and system test information for engineers on the ground.

sub_modules.title: Display Select Panel
sub_modules.content: Controls EICAS functions including engine display, status display, event recording, computer selection, brightness, thrust reference, and reset functions.

sub_modules.title: Event Recording
sub_modules.content: Stores automatic and manual fault data in system memory for later analysis.

sub_modules.title: Alert Message Levels
sub_modules.content: Level A (Warning): immediate action, red. Level B (Caution): amber with tone. Level C (Advisory): amber without tone.

sub_modules.title: Alert Message Handling
sub_modules.content: Messages are prioritized, can be cancelled or recalled, and automatically removed when conditions normalize.

sub_modules.title: Display Paging
sub_modules.content: If messages exceed display capacity, additional pages are created and accessed via controls.

sub_modules.title: Display Unit Failure
sub_modules.content: Failure of a display unit causes data transfer to remaining unit in compact format.

sub_modules.title: Standby Engine Indicator
sub_modules.content: Provides essential engine data (N1, N2, EGT) in case of total EICAS failure.

sub_modules.title: ECAM System Overview
sub_modules.content: ECAM displays aircraft system data in checklist and schematic formats, focusing on system operation and corrective actions.

sub_modules.title: ECAM Display Units
sub_modules.content: Left display shows warnings and procedures; right display shows system schematics.

sub_modules.title: ECAM Display Modes
sub_modules.content: Includes flight phase-related, advisory/status, failure-related, and manual system display modes.

sub_modules.title: Flight Phase Mode
sub_modules.content: Automatically displays relevant data for each phase of flight such as preflight, take-off, cruise, and landing.

sub_modules.title: Advisory and Status Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays aircraft condition summaries and operational limitations.

sub_modules.title: Failure-related Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays system failures, corrective actions, and associated system diagrams with highest priority.

sub_modules.title: Manual System Display Mode
sub_modules.content: Allows manual selection of system diagrams for monitoring and checks.

sub_modules.title: ECAM Control Panel
sub_modules.content: Includes controls for system display selection, clearing messages, recalling alerts, and system isolation.

sub_modules.title: Synoptic Displays
sub_modules.content: Graphical system representations used for monitoring aircraft systems.

sub_modules.title: Alert Handling in ECAM
sub_modules.content: Warnings trigger visual, aural alerts and guide pilots through corrective procedures step-by-step.
